title: Agent session registry managing child processes and permissions
purpose: Maintains a registry of agent sessions, handling child process lifecycle, permission management, and session persistence.
module_context: Exports a registry class for managing agent sessions with permission bridge integration, worktree management, and Claude session ID generation using UUIDv5. Includes utility functions for UUID conversion and path extraction.

steps.content: Remove hyphens from the input UUID string.
Create a new 16-byte array to hold the result.
Convert each pair of hex characters into a byte value and store in the output array.
Convert each byte in the input array to a two-character hex string.
Join all hex characters into a single string.
Insert hyphens at the standard UUID positions to format the result.
Convert the namespace UUID to a byte array.
Create a SHA-1 hash of the namespace bytes concatenated with the input name.
Take the first 16 bytes of the hash digest.
Set the version bits to 5 and the variant bits to RFC 4122 compliant value.
Convert the resulting byte array back to a UUID string format.
Initialize the persistence directory path from options or default to .collab/agent-sessions.
Initialize the project root path from options or default to current working directory.
Create a permission bridge instance with broadcast handler and configuration from options.
Create a worktree manager instance with project root and persistence configuration.

methods.name: resolvePermission
methods.normalized_params: (sessionId: string, promptId: string, decision: PermissionDecision, userLabel?: string): void

steps.content: Delegate the permission resolution to the internal permission bridge instance.
Retrieve the session entry from the registry map.
IF the session exists, update its permission mode.
IF the bridge has a setMode function, delegate the mode change to it.
Check if an existing session entry is present and alive.
IF an existing session is found and alive, return it immediately.
Check if a pending start promise exists for this session.
IF a pending start exists, return the existing promise.
Start a new child process for the session and track the promise.
Return the promise that resolves to the new child manager.
Read the persisted session record from storage.
IF no record is found, throw an error indicating the session does not exist.
ELSE use the persisted working directory to get or create the session.
Retrieve the session entry from the registry map.
IF no entry exists, return immediately.
Stop the child process associated with the session.
Cancel any pending permission requests for the session.
Close the permission socket server if it exists.
Remove the session entry from the registry map and transcript storage.
Stop the session and clean up associated resources.
Attempt to remove the worktree associated with the session.
IF worktree removal fails, log a warning but continue.
Attempt to delete the persisted session file.
IF file deletion fails, ignore the error and continue.

methods.name: runCommitPushPR
methods.normalized_params: (sessionId: string, opts: { title: string; body?: string; draft?: boolean }): Promise<void>

steps.content: Retrieve the session entry from the registry map.
IF no worktree exists or it is non-git, throw an error.
Generate unique identifiers for the turn and tool use.
Dispatch turn start and tool call started events to the session.
Execute the commit and push pull request operation using the worktree manager.
IF the operation succeeds, dispatch a tool call completed event with success status.
IF the operation fails, dispatch a tool call completed event with error status.
Dispatch turn end event to conclude the operation.
Retrieve the transcript for the specified session from storage.
IF no transcript exists, return an empty array.
ELSE return the stored transcript array.
Retrieve the session entry from the registry map.
IF the entry exists and the child is alive, return the child manager.
ELSE return null.
Dispatch the event to the session's channel.
Retrieve the session entry from the registry map.
IF no entry exists, return immediately.
Cancel the current turn in the child process.
Cancel any pending permission requests for the session.
Reset the projection context for the session.
Dispatch a turn end event with canceled status.
Generate a file path for the Claude session JSONL file.
Attempt to access the file path to check if it exists.
IF the file exists, return true.
IF the file does not exist, return false.
Generate a Claude session ID for the session.
Set the default permission mode for the session.
Create a socket directory and generate a socket path.
Generate a settings file for the session.
Ensure a worktree exists for the session.
Check if the worktree is dirty.
Determine the working directory for spawning the child process.
Check if a Claude session exists for resuming.
Dispatch worktree information event to the session.
Start a permission socket server for handling permission requests.
Create a child manager instance with session configuration.
Initialize a projection context for the session.
Set up event listeners for stdout frames, stderr, exit, and error.
Start the child process.
Write a persistence record for the session.
Store the session entry in the registry map.
Dispatch a session started event to the session.
IF resuming, backfill historical events from Claude session.

methods.name: backfillHistory
methods.normalized_params: (sessionId: string, cwd: string, claudeSessionId: string): Promise<void>

steps.content: Generate a file path for the Claude session JSONL file.
Read the contents of the JSONL file.
IF the file cannot be read, return immediately.
Split the file contents into lines.
Process each line as a JSON entry.
IF the entry is a user message, dispatch a user message event.
IF the entry is an assistant message, dispatch turn start, tool calls, and assistant message events.
IF the entry is an assistant message, dispatch a turn end event.
For any tool calls that completed without results, dispatch tool call completed events with error status.
Generate a channel name for the session.
Broadcast the agent event to the channel.
Add the event to the session's transcript ring buffer.
If the ring buffer exceeds maximum size, remove the oldest entry.
Generate a SHA-1 hash of the session ID.
Construct a file path using the hash and persistence directory.
Generate the persistence file path for the session.
Attempt to read the persistence file.
IF the file does not exist, return null.
IF reading fails for other reasons, return null.
Parse the file contents as JSON and return the record.
Ensure the persistence directory exists.
Write the record to the persistence file in JSON format.
IF the tool name is not provided or input is not an object, return an empty array.
IF the tool name is Read, Edit, Write, MultiEdit, or NotebookEdit, extract file path from input.
IF the tool name is Grep or Glob, extract path from input.
ELSE return an empty array.

methods.name: isNonGit
methods.normalized_params: (wt: SessionWorktree): wt is { kind: 'non_git'; sessionId: string; path: string }

steps.content: Check if the worktree has a kind property equal to 'non_git'.
IF the path is not absolute, return false.
Resolve the input path and worktree path to absolute paths.
Check if the resolved input path matches or is a subdirectory of the resolved worktree path.
